title: Crispy Smoky Swede Stir-fry
description: A cozy Norwegian dish featuring crispy swede (also known as rutabaga) cooked in a smoky sauce with a touch of spice and served as a side or light meal.

ingredients.name: Swede
ingredients.notes: Choose ones that are firm with some sweetness.

ingredients.name: Smoky Sauce Mix (optional)
ingredients.amount: Follow instructions on the packet of sauce or use your favorite blend.

ingredients.name: Rice Wine Vinegar or Apple Cider Vinegar

instructions: Wash the swede and peel it. Slice into thin strips, about 0.5 cm thick.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add swede slices and cook until they start to turn golden brown on one side, then flip them over.
Add minced garlic to the pan and stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
If using a smoky sauce mix, add it now and stir well into the swede mixture. If not, continue with the next step.
Season with salt, pepper, and chili flakes to taste. Stir again to combine seasoning evenly throughout the dish.
Add Nutritional Yeast for extra umami flavor if desired.
Pour in vinegar and stir until the liquid is absorbed by the swede, creating a slightly thicker sauce consistency.
Remove from heat once all ingredients are fully combined and cooked through. Serve hot.
